_meta.purpose: Post-mortem structured SOW parse for DARPA HR0011SB20254 Topic R12 (ASEMA). This is the SEED FAILING CASE for the future grants-specialist agent's gate-0/gate-1 pipeline. Pair with the NASA Appendix 2026A parse (forthcoming) as the seed PASSING CASE.
_meta.parser: manual (strategist scaffolded; koda corrected hallucinated topic_title and unverified faq_url on extraction to cairn/proposals/)
_meta.outcome_summary: FAIL at gate-0 (phase_eligibility) AND gate-1 (scope_exclusions ∩ proposed_pitch). Withdraw and redirect to NASA SBIR 26-27 Appendix 2026A.
extraction_corrections: topic_title fixed: was 'Adaptive Secure Encrypted Messaging Architecture (ASEMA)' (hallucinated); actual per archive/asema-2026-04/vol2_v1.md line 3 is 'Assessing Security of Encrypted Messaging Applications (ASEMA)'
faq_url marked [CONFIRM]: strategist scaffolded a plausible-pattern URL that was not independently verified. Verify against DARPA SBIR portal before this record seeds the grants-agent test set.

opportunity.topic_title: Assessing Security of Encrypted Messaging Applications (ASEMA)
opportunity.primary_doc_url: [CONFIRM: David — DARPA SBIR portal direct topic URL]
opportunity.faq_url: [CONFIRM: David — strategist scaffolded https://www.darpa.mil/sites/default/files/attachment/2025-12/faq-hr0011sb20254-12-4.pdf based on URL pattern; not independently verified]

evidence.quote_paraphrase: DARPA confirmed Topic R12 is open to Direct-to-Phase-II (DP2) submissions only. No Phase I submissions accepted.
evidence.verbatim_required: [CONFIRM: David — extract verbatim FAQ Q23 text into this field for the agent training set]

phase_eligibility.komatik_reason: Komatik does not have prior Phase-I-equivalent funded work that would qualify for DP2 under DARPA's feasibility-evidence requirements.

scope_inclusions: [CONFIRM: David — extract from R12 SOW text the explicit 'in scope' enumeration: messaging-architecture adaptation, threat-model coverage, post-quantum readiness language, etc.]

scope_exclusions.language: Cryptographic-protocol design and analysis (key exchange, primitives, formal protocol verification at the wire-format level) is OUT of scope.

evidence.quote_paraphrase: DARPA explicitly excluded cryptographic-protocol focus from the R12 work scope. Proposers focusing on protocol design or analysis will be deemed non-responsive.
evidence.verbatim_required: [CONFIRM: David — extract verbatim FAQ Q1 text]

scope_exclusions.language: [CONFIRM: David — re-read SOW for any other named exclusions: e.g. 'this topic is not interested in...', 'proposers should NOT submit...', 'out of scope:']

evidence_requirements: [CONFIRM: David — DP2 feasibility evidence requirements: prior funded work, demonstration milestones, IP position]
[CONFIRM: David — any letters of intent, customer commitments, or transition partners required by SOW]
evaluation_criteria: [CONFIRM: David — extract from SOW the verbatim evaluation criteria with weights, in order: typically Technical Approach > Commercial Potential > Personnel > Cost Reasonableness]

submission_constraints.page_limit: [CONFIRM: David — DARPA Phase II tech volume page limit]

proposed_pitch.anchored_on: Signal Protocol-style ratcheted messaging with audit-bundle layer (Trace) for defender-actionable forensics on cryptographic-protocol behavior
proposed_pitch.draft_artifact: ../vol2_v1.md (preserved for archive value only — not reusable as written)

fail_modes.result: FAIL — pitch is structured for Phase I (DP1); R12 accepts DP2 only
FAIL — pitch primary anchor is cryptographic-protocol analysis (Signal Protocol layer), which is in scope_exclusions[exclusion_crypto_protocols]

exclusion_conflicts.trace_exclusion: Trace is not a SAST/DAST replacement; not a SIEM; not a vulnerability scanner. It is a layer above those tools producing audit-actionable artifacts.
exclusion_conflicts.note: The Trace capability itself does not violate scope. The PROPOSED PITCH violated scope by anchoring Trace on cryptographic-protocol forensics — a use-case that pulls Trace into the excluded zone. Lesson: capability fit is necessary but not sufficient; the framing of the application matters.

decision.decision_rationale: Two independent disqualifiers: phase ineligibility and scope-exclusion conflict. Either alone would justify withdraw; together they are conclusive.

redirect_target.subtopic: Technologies that improve the safety, efficiency, and management of airspace operations
redirect_target.rationale: Vector capability has direct keyword fit ('airspace operations'), Phase I eligibility, and PI ATC operational background as evaluation differentiator.

lessons_for_pipeline: Phase eligibility check (gate-0) MUST run BEFORE any drafting effort. Cost: ~30 sec parse vs. ~40 hrs of dead authoring.
Scope-exclusion check (gate-1) requires reading FAQs and Q&A docs in addition to primary SOW. Solicitations frequently use FAQs to narrow scope after publication.

Capability-fit keyword match alone is insufficient. The PITCH FRAMING can pull a valid capability into an excluded zone — gate-1 must check pitch anchor against exclusions, not just capability tags against inclusions.

Failing case structure: this record is the canonical failing-case shape. Future grants-agent should produce records with this exact schema for every parsed opportunity, regardless of go/no-go outcome.
Confirm verbatim quotes: agent must extract verbatim SOW/FAQ text into evidence fields, not paraphrase. Paraphrase is acceptable for human review but not for agent re-training.
open_evidence_extraction: Full SOW text for in-scope and exclusion enumeration
